element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player navigates pirate ships and cannon mechanics
Navigate complex terrain with minecart sequences

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Space bonus stage (if all stars collected)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Final spaceship sequence leading to endgame mech boss
Defeat final mech boss; weight-based puzzle solution
Unlocks Western, Dinosaur, and Sci-Fi levels; better ending

typical_tension_triggers: Increasing vertical complexity in horror/space sections
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation from simple pirate navigation to complex environmental platforming (adventure) to atmospheric horror, culminating in high-intensity space/mech finale for completionists. Difficulty spikes at boss encounters.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden stars scattered throughout levels; finding all five per level unlocks bonus content (Western, Dinosaur, Sci-Fi movies) and alternative ending
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Stars serve as hidden collectible gates to unlock bonus movie levels and true ending

difficulty_curve.early_game: Pirate Movie introduces basic platforming and combat fundamentals with manageable enemy encounters; Giant Octopus serves as introductory boss with moderate challenge
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Adventure Movie escalates environmental complexity with canyons, minecart sequences, temple navigation, and Giant Spider boss; requires mastery of established mechanics
difficulty_curve.late_game: Horror Movie emphasizes vertical navigation (graveyard, large staircase) with increased spatial disorientation; conditional endgame access based on star collection leads to Space stage and final Giant Robot Mech boss
notable_spikes: Large staircase section (horror movie vertical challenge)
Giant Robot Mech boss (final boss endgame spike)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each major level represents a distinct film genre (pirate, adventure, horror, sci-fi) with thematic environmental and enemy design. Gameplay mechanics reflect genre conventions (cannons in pirate film, temples in adventure, graveyard in horror).
Each movie concludes with a genre-appropriate boss encounter that represents the film's climax. Giant creatures tied to thematic content (octopus for pirate, spider for adventure, mech for sci-fi).
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All movies; Giant Octopus (Pirate), Giant Spider (Adventure), Giant Robot Mech (Space/Sci-Fi)

notable_level_patterns.description: Five hidden stars per level serve as completion gates. Collecting all stars across all levels unlocks bonus movie levels (Western, Dinosaur) and accesses true ending via Space/endgame stage.
Game employs isometric graphics throughout, creating depth-judgment challenges that affect platforming precision. Reviewers noted this makes ledge placement and object visibility difficult to assess.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels (particularly notable in Saturn/PS versions with full isometric graphics)
